enonce.texte: Match the health vocabulary words with their correct definitions. This exercise will help you understand key terms used in health campaigns and prevention.

questions.texte: Match each word to its definition.

sousQuestions.texte: Vaccine
sousQuestions.reponse: A substance that helps the body fight a specific disease.

sousQuestions.texte: Protection
sousQuestions.reponse: The act of keeping someone or something safe from harm.

sousQuestions.texte: Hygiene
sousQuestions.reponse: Practices that help maintain health and prevent disease.

sousQuestions.texte: Symptoms
sousQuestions.reponse: Physical signs that indicate the presence of a disease.

sousQuestions.texte: Treatment
sousQuestions.reponse: Medical care given to cure a disease or heal an injury.

enonce.texte:
Read the following text about a health campaign and answer the questions below.

"The Ministry of Health has launched a new campaign to fight against malaria in rural areas. The campaign, called 'Zero Malaria', aims to distribute free mosquito nets and educate communities about prevention methods. Health workers will visit villages to provide information about symptoms and treatment. They will also encourage people to keep their environment clean to prevent the breeding of mosquitoes. The campaign started in June and will continue until September, which is the rainy season when malaria cases usually increase."

questions.texte: Answer the following comprehension questions about the text.

sousQuestions.texte: Identify the name of the health campaign.
sousQuestions.reponse: The campaign is called 'Zero Malaria'.

sousQuestions.texte: Describe what the campaign aims to do.
sousQuestions.reponse: The campaign aims to distribute free mosquito nets and educate communities about prevention methods against malaria.

sousQuestions.texte: Analyze why the campaign is held from June to September.
sousQuestions.reponse: The campaign is held from June to September because this is the rainy season when malaria cases usually increase.

enonce.texte: Conditional sentences (Type 1) are used to talk about real and possible situations. The structure is: If + present simple, will + infinitive (or modal + infinitive).

questions.texte: Complete the sentences using the correct form of the verbs in brackets.

sousQuestions.texte: If you (take) ______ the vaccine, you (be) ______ better protected.
sousQuestions.reponse: If you take the vaccine, you will be better protected.

sousQuestions.texte: If people (wash) ______ their hands regularly, they (reduce) ______ the spread of germs.
sousQuestions.reponse: If people wash their hands regularly, they will reduce the spread of germs.

sousQuestions.texte: If the government (invest) ______ more in healthcare, many lives (save) ______.
sousQuestions.reponse: If the government invests more in healthcare, many lives will be saved.

intitule: Health Tips for a School Magazine

enonce.texte:
Read the medical brochure excerpt below about disease prevention. Then write a list of health tips for your school magazine.

"COVID-19 Prevention Tips: Wear a mask in public places. Wash your hands frequently with soap and water. Maintain social distancing. Avoid crowded areas. Get vaccinated when possible. If you have symptoms like fever or cough, stay home and seek medical attention."

questions.texte: Write a list of health tips for a school magazine.

sousQuestions.texte: List 5 health tips from the text in your own words.
sousQuestions.reponse: 1. Wear a mask in public places. 2. Wash your hands frequently. 3. Practice social distancing. 4. Avoid crowded areas. 5. Get vaccinated.

sousQuestions.texte: Create a title for your health tips list.
sousQuestions.reponse: Example: 'Top 5 Tips to Stay Safe from COVID-19'

enonce.texte:
Read the following health advice and answer the questions.

"To prevent the spread of infectious diseases, you should always cover your mouth when you cough. You must also wash your hands regularly. If you feel unwell, you should see a doctor. You don't have to go to the hospital for every minor illness, but you should not ignore serious symptoms."

questions.texte: Analyze the health advice and answer the questions.

sousQuestions.texte: Identify the recommendation given for coughing.
sousQuestions.reponse: You should always cover your mouth when you cough.

sousQuestions.texte: Explain why it is important to wash hands regularly according to the text.
sousQuestions.reponse: Washing hands regularly is important to prevent the spread of infectious diseases.

sousQuestions.texte: Evaluate the statement: 'You don't have to go to the hospital for every minor illness.' Do you agree? Justify your answer.
sousQuestions.reponse: Personal answer. Example: 'I agree because for minor illnesses like a cold, rest and home remedies are often sufficient. However, it is important to monitor symptoms and seek medical help if they worsen.'

enonce.texte: You are part of a school health club. Your mission is to create a simple health campaign poster about disease prevention.

questions.texte: Create a poster to promote disease prevention.

sousQuestions.texte: Choose a disease (e.g., malaria, COVID-19, cholera) to focus on.
sousQuestions.reponse: Example: Malaria

sousQuestions.texte: Write a catchy slogan for your campaign.
sousQuestions.reponse: Example: 'Fight Malaria - Protect Your Community!'

sousQuestions.texte: List 3 prevention tips in simple language.
sousQuestions.reponse: Example: 1. Sleep under a mosquito net. 2. Use insect repellent. 3. Remove standing water.

sousQuestions.texte: Design a simple visual layout for your poster.
sousQuestions.reponse: Example layout: Title at the top, a simple drawing of a mosquito or protective measures in the center, and prevention tips at the bottom.

enonce.texte: Conditional Type 1 sentences are used to talk about real and possible situations. Practice using them in a health context.

sousQuestions.texte: If you (not get) ______ enough sleep, you (feel) ______ tired the next day.
sousQuestions.reponse: If you don't get enough sleep, you will feel tired the next day.

sousQuestions.texte: If the school (provide) ______ free masks, more students (protect) ______ themselves.
sousQuestions.reponse: If the school provides free masks, more students will protect themselves.

sousQuestions.texte: If people (practise) ______ good hygiene, the spread of disease (reduce) ______ significantly.
sousQuestions.reponse: If people practise good hygiene, the spread of disease will be reduced significantly.

enonce.texte: You are a health ambassador. Write a short health campaign message for your community.

questions.texte: Write a short health campaign message.

sousQuestions.texte: Start with a greeting to your community.
sousQuestions.reponse: Example: 'Dear community members,'

sousQuestions.texte: State the health issue you are addressing.
sousQuestions.reponse: Example: 'We are facing a serious health challenge: the spread of cholera.'

sousQuestions.texte: Give 3 pieces of advice using modals (should, have to, must).
sousQuestions.reponse: Example: 'You should drink clean water. You must wash your hands regularly. You have to keep your environment clean.'

sousQuestions.texte: Conclude with a message of hope or encouragement.
sousQuestions.reponse: Example: 'Together, we can stop the spread of disease and build a healthier community. Stay safe!'

enonce.texte: Type 1 conditional sentences have two parts: the 'if' clause (condition) and the main clause (result). They express real and possible situations.

questions.texte: Create conditional sentences by matching the halves.

sousQuestions.texte: Match: 'If you eat healthy food'
sousQuestions.reponse: You will have more energy.

sousQuestions.texte: Match: 'If you don't drink enough water'
sousQuestions.reponse: You will become dehydrated.

sousQuestions.texte: Match: 'If the government invests in healthcare'
sousQuestions.reponse: The population will be healthier.

enonce.texte:
Read the following health campaign summary and evaluate its effectiveness.

"The 'Wash Your Hands' campaign was launched in schools last year. It included posters in bathrooms, weekly handwashing demonstrations, and free soap distribution. After six months, the number of students visiting the school nurse for stomach illnesses decreased by 40%."

questions.texte: Evaluate the health campaign based on the information provided.

sousQuestions.texte: Identify the activities included in the campaign.
sousQuestions.reponse: The campaign included posters in bathrooms, weekly handwashing demonstrations, and free soap distribution.

sousQuestions.texte: Analyze the results of the campaign.
sousQuestions.reponse: After six months, the number of students visiting the school nurse for stomach illnesses decreased by 40%, indicating a positive impact.

sousQuestions.texte: Evaluate the effectiveness of the campaign. What made it successful?
sousQuestions.reponse: The campaign was effective because it combined education (demonstrations and posters) with practical support (free soap). This approach raised awareness and made it easier for students to practice good hygiene.
